level_signals: 6+ years of product management experience or equivalent
Owns end-to-end enterprise platform with multi-year vision responsibility
Engages directly with enterprise users and external forums

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience or equivalent
Experience working on B2B products, ideally with a track record of building for external users
Technically adept systems thinker, ideally with an engineering degree strongly preferred.
Great interpersonal skills and strong verbal and written communication. You can make complex concepts accessible with clear communication.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Demonstrated success and strong execution track record in building payment products

scope.surface: Stripe Terminal Unified Commerce platform — end-to-end in-person payments platform for global enterprises
primary_metrics_inferred: Terminal revenue growth / enterprise user adoption
core_responsibilities: Own Stripe's unified commerce platform for Enterprise users worldwide
Operate at both a massive and nuanced scale, identifying holistic investments for Stripe as well as specific needs for users
Represent Terminal and Stripe in external forums like front of users: directly engaging in product discovery and roadmapping conversations
Set a motivating multi-year vision and then break it down into measurable, meaningful milestones for the Terminal Enterprise team
Creating buy-in, crafting roadmaps and OKRs, and navigating trade-offs

Engage with various teams at Stripe like product partnerships, product marketing, partner marketing, product legal and vertical industry teams to collaborate on user and partner needs, industry events, and GTM motions

green_flags: Terminal described as one of the fastest growing products at Stripe — high visibility role
Clear product surface and ownership (end-to-end unified commerce platform)
Multi-year vision scope signals significant strategic ownership
Cross-functional stakeholder engagement with executive-level exposure implied
red_flags: No location or remote policy specified anywhere in the JD — candidates cannot assess geography fit
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Engineering degree described as 'strongly preferred' but framed as a 'nice to have' qualifier — ambiguous classification

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (set to 'unclear' — no location data in JD)
compensation.source (unknown — no compensation data in JD)
ambiguities: 'Technically adept systems thinker, ideally with an engineering degree strongly preferred.' — phrasing uses 'ideally' and 'strongly preferred' but appears in the main 'We're looking for someone who has' section (must-haves). Treated as hard/must-have with the credential as a soft signal within it.

'Experience working on B2B products, ideally with a track record of building for external users' — 'ideally' qualifier softens this; however it appears in the required section so classified as hard severity.
